title: The Baking Class
introKo:
동네 베이킹 클래스에서 반죽을 배우는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

0.name: 수줍은 견습생
0.en: At home, you eat one warm bun with a cup of tea and save some for tomorrow. All week, you practice quietly in your own kitchen, just you and the dough. Next Saturday, you arrive early and take a seat in the back row.
0.ko: 집에 와서 따뜻한 번 하나를 차 한 잔과 함께 먹고, 나머지는 내일 몫으로 아껴 둬요. 일주일 내내 집 부엌에서 조용히 연습해요. 반죽과 단둘이서요. 다음 토요일엔 일찍 도착해서 뒷줄 자리에 앉아요.

1.name: 신중한 모범생
1.en: Every Saturday, you take the same seat in the middle row and follow each step exactly the way Miss Sugar shows you. Your bread rises a little higher every week. "Good hands learn fast," she says, and you almost believe her.
1.ko: 매주 토요일, 가운데 줄 같은 자리에 앉아 미스 슈가가 보여 주는 대로 한 단계씩 정확히 따라 해요. 빵은 매주 조금씩 더 높이 부풀어요. "좋은 손은 빨리 배워요"라고 선생님이 말하는데, 이제 거의 믿을 것 같아요.

2.name: 리듬을 아는 제빵사
2.en: The next week, you bring your own apron and take the table closest to Miss Sugar. "Someone came ready," she says with a smile. Your dough rises faster than all the others, and you both pretend not to notice.
2.ko: 다음 주엔 앞치마까지 챙겨 가서 미스 슈가와 가장 가까운 작업대를 골라요. "준비하고 왔네요." 선생님이 웃으며 말해요. 당신의 반죽은 누구 것보다 빨리 부풀고, 두 사람 다 모르는 척해요.

3.name: 오븐보다 뜨거운 수강생
3.en: A month later, you are Miss Sugar's private student, and the evening classes are just the two of you. The little kitchen gets hotter every week, and she says you never get used to the heat. You are no longer sure she is talking about the oven.
3.ko: 한 달 뒤, 당신은 미스 슈가의 개인 수강생이 돼요. 저녁 수업은 단둘뿐이고, 작은 주방은 매주 더 뜨거워져요. 열기엔 절대 익숙해지지 않는다고 선생님은 말하는데, 그게 정말 오븐 얘기인지 이제 잘 모르겠어요.

normal.text: It is Saturday morning. You walk into a small baking class in your neighborhood. The air smells warm and sweet.
The teacher, Miss Sugar, gives you a big smile. "A new student," she says. "Fresh hands. I love fresh hands."
"Today we make bread," she says. "Good bread needs three things: warm hands, strong arms, and a lot of patience."

options.en: I'm ready. My hands are always warm.
options.reaction: "Perfect," she says. "Warm hands make the dough very happy."

options.ko: 저 한 번도 빵을 구워 본 적이 없어요.
options.reaction: "Even better," she smiles. "I love teaching beginners. We will take it slowly."

normal.text: Miss Sugar puts soft dough in front of you. "Now knead it," she says. "Push with both hands. Don't be shy — this dough likes a firm touch."
You push and fold, push and fold. "Slower," she says. "Find a rhythm. Feel how it changes under your hands."

options.en: I've found my rhythm. Watch me.
options.reaction: "Exactly like that. Slow and steady," she says. "You have very good hands."

options.en: My arms are getting tired already.
options.reaction: "Already? Don't stop now," she laughs. "The dough knows when you give up."

normal.text: "Now we let it rest," she says. "Cover it and keep it warm. Soon it will rise. It always rises for me."
The oven is on, and the room grows hot. Miss Sugar fans her face. "This heat," she says. "You never get used to it. But without heat, nothing happens."
You shape the dough into small round buns. "Oh my," Miss Sugar says softly. "Lovely buns. Some of the best I have seen in this class."

options.en: It's all thanks to your teaching.
options.ko: 다 선생님이 잘 가르쳐 주신 덕분이에요.
options.reaction: "Oh, stop," she laughs. "The hands did all the work. And what hands."

options.en: Baking is more fun than I expected.
options.reaction: "It always is," she says. "Careful — once you start, it is very hard to stop."

normal.text: The buns come out of the oven, golden and warm. Miss Sugar hands you a bag of cream. "Now the filling. Squeeze slowly, with steady pressure. If you hurry, it goes everywhere."
You walk home with a box of warm cream buns. There is flour on your shirt and a smile on your face. "Same time next week?" Miss Sugar calls. You have already signed up.

hard.text: On a quiet Saturday morning, you step into a small baking class in your neighborhood, where the air is thick with the smell of warm sugar.
The teacher, Miss Sugar, greets you with a bright smile. "A new student," she says. "Fresh hands. There is nothing I love more than fresh hands."
"Today we are making bread," she announces. "Good bread asks for three things: warm hands, strong arms, and more patience than most people expect."

options.en: I'm ready. My hands are always warm.
options.reaction: "Perfect," she says. "You would be surprised how much difference warm hands make to the dough."

options.en: Okay, but I have never baked anything before.
options.ko: 네, 근데 저 한 번도 빵을 구워 본 적이 없어요.
options.reaction: "Even better," she smiles. "There is nothing I enjoy more than teaching a complete beginner. We will take it nice and slowly."

hard.text: Miss Sugar sets a ball of soft dough in front of you. "Now knead it," she says. "Push with both hands and put your weight into it. Don't be shy — this dough responds to a firm touch."
You push and fold, push and fold. "Slower," she murmurs. "Find a rhythm you can keep all day. Feel how it softens under your hands."

options.en: I believe I've found my rhythm. Watch me closely.
options.ko: 리듬을 찾은 것 같아요. 가까이서 봐 주세요.
options.reaction: "Exactly like that. Slow and steady," she says approvingly. "You have remarkably good hands."

options.en: My arms are getting tired already.
options.reaction: "Already? Don't you dare stop now," she laughs. "The dough always knows when you give up."

hard.text: "Now we let it rest," she says, covering the bowl with a cloth. "Keep it warm and be patient. Soon it will rise. It has never once failed to rise for me."
The oven has been on for a while, and the little room grows hot. Miss Sugar fans her face. "This heat," she sighs. "You never quite get used to it. But without heat, nothing ever happens in here."
You shape the risen dough into small round buns. "Oh my," Miss Sugar says softly, tilting her head. "Lovely buns. Easily some of the finest I have seen in this class."

options.en: It's all thanks to your teaching.
options.ko: 다 선생님이 잘 가르쳐 주신 덕분이에요.
options.reaction: "Oh, stop," she laughs, waving a hand. "Your hands did all the work. And what hands they are."

options.en: Baking is far more fun than I expected.
options.reaction: "It always is," she says knowingly. "Careful — once you start, it becomes very hard to stop."

hard.text: The buns come out of the oven golden and warm. Miss Sugar hands you a bag of cream. "Now for the filling," she says. "Squeeze slowly, with steady pressure. Rush it, and the cream goes absolutely everywhere."
You walk home with a box of warm cream buns, flour still dusting your shirt. "Same time next week?" Miss Sugar calls from the doorway. You had signed up before she even finished the question.

wink.knead: 반죽을 치대다, 주무르다 — '주무르다'가 꼭 반죽에만 쓰는 말은 아니죠.
wink.firm: 단단한, 확실한 — 반죽 말고 다른 걸 만질 때의 손길 얘기로 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.rhythm: 리듬 — 반죽에만 필요한 건 아니라는 말투로 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.rise: 부풀어 오르다 — 빵 말고 다른 것도 일어선다는 뜻으로 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.hot: 뜨거운 — 오븐 온도만 말하는 게 아닐 수도 있어요.
wink.buns: 둥근 빵 — 빵이 아닌 다른 둥근 것을 칭찬하는 걸로 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.squeeze: 짜다, 쥐다 — 천천히 일정한 압력으로 하라는 조언이 묘하게 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.cream: 크림 — '천천히, 일정한 압력으로 짜세요'라는 조언과 만나면 빵 얘기로만 들리지 않아요.

introEn:
A neighborhood baking class, perfectly wholesome.
...on the surface. Tap words to see the hidden meaning.
